name: Slack Addressee Guard
description: Pre-send guard that rewrites an agent reply leading with an accidental Slack @-mention when a different human spoke last in the thread.

enabled.label: Enable Slack Addressee Guard
enabled.help: When enabled, agent replies leading with the target user's @-mention are rewritten to address the last human in the thread if they were different.

mode.label: Mode
mode.help: rewrite (default) repairs the leading address. cancel drops the reply entirely when a mismatch is detected.

targetUserId.label: Target Slack User ID
targetUserId.help: The Slack user id the agent too often addresses by default (e.g. the operator). Replies leading with this id are inspected.

botUserId.label: Bot Slack User ID
botUserId.help: The Slack user id of the bot itself. Used to skip bot messages when walking thread history to find the last human.

humanNames.label: Human Name Map
humanNames.help: Optional map of Slack user ids to display names for log records.

logPath.label: Log Path
logPath.help: Optional JSONL path for decisions. Defaults to no file logging when omitted.

cacheTtlMs.label: Thread Cache TTL (ms)
cacheTtlMs.help: How long a resolved last-human-in-thread lookup is cached. Defaults to 8s.

allowlistPatterns.label: Allowlist Regex Patterns
allowlistPatterns.help: Optional additional regex patterns (JS flavor) that mark a reply as a cron/PM/nudge header that must stay intact.

askedForTargetPhrases.label: Asked-For-Target Phrases
askedForTargetPhrases.help: Optional additional phrases that mean the last human explicitly requested the target user be addressed.
